title: Review and setlist: Leslie Odom Jr. brings Broadway to the Boston Pops
author: Shannon Murphy
bullets: Leslie Odom Jr. performed with the Boston Pops at Symphony Hall as part of their 2026 spring season.
The concert featured a mix of American classics and Broadway hits, including 'Fly Me To The Moon' and 'Wait For It.'
Odom Jr.'s performance included songs from Hamilton, along with his interpretation of 'A Change is Gonna Come'.

title: Chelsea man facing slew of assault charges for alleged violence against young children
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Chelsea man Kener Bautista-Zepeda, 19, faces 12 charges related to assaults on two children under 14.
The charges include assault with a dangerous weapon, substantial injury, reckless endangerment, and strangulation.
Bautista-Zepeda was arrested in Virginia after fleeing Massachusetts and is held without bail pending a May 28 hearing.

title: Concord man, 73, convicted of raping 20-year-old in Sandwich restaurant bathroom in 2023
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Concord man Elie Jammal, 73, convicted of raping a 20-year-old in a Sandwich restaurant bathroom in 2023.
Jammal sentenced to 13 ½ to 15 years in prison for rape, plus 5 years probation for indecent assault.
Court cited Jammal's actions as motivated by a 'shocking sense of entitlement,' with no intoxication involved.

title: Newton Church, founded in 1893, is becoming condos
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Newton's Church of the Open Word will be converted into six condos and a separate five-story, 26-unit building.
Developer Habitech Communities bought the former church in 2024 for $4 million after a failed arts center proposal.
This conversion reflects a trend in Boston of repurposing churches into residences amid shrinking congregations.

title: 5 players the Celtics could target this offseason outside of Giannis Antetokounmpo
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: The Celtics could target Kawhi Leonard, available for trade, adding championship experience and defensive versatility to the roster.
LeBron James, an unrestricted free agent, might join the Celtics if he seeks a final title run outside Los Angeles.
John Collins, Jaxson Hayes, and Coby White are other possible targets offering frontcourt depth and scoring options.

title: 2 local college baseball programs advance to NCAA World Series
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Bentley University advanced to the NCAA Division II World Series for the first time after defeating Molloy University.
Endicott College heads to its fourth consecutive NCAA Division III championship after beating Salve Regina University.
Bentley won Game 3 in dramatic fashion, and Endicott secured victory with a decisive 12-4 win in Game 3.

title: Celtics reportedly plan to work out UConn basketball star ahead of NBA Draft
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: The Boston Celtics plan to work out UConn star Alex Karaban ahead of the 2026 NBA Draft.
Karaban, a Southborough native, is ranked No. 32 on ESPN's draft prospects list and played four years at UConn.
The Celtics have the No. 27 and No. 40 picks in the 2026 NBA Draft, with Karaban as a potential candidate.

title: Review & setlist: Charlie Puth turns MGM Music Hall into a masterclass in musicianship
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Charlie Puth performed at MGM Music Hall in Fenway on May 22, showcasing his jazz-influenced musicianship.
The concert featured a blend of older hits and new songs from his March 2026 album 'Whatever’s Clever!'.
Puth interacted with the Boston audience, reminiscing about his Berklee days and sharing personal stories.

title: Review and setlist: Jakob Dylan’s choice to channel Tom Petty pays off at Beverly Wallflowers show
author: Peter Chianca
bullets: The Wallflowers performed their 1996 album 'Bringing Down the Horse' in full at The Cabot in Beverly.
Jakob Dylan's unique rendition of Tom Petty's 'Long After Dark' album followed, highlighting the show's setlist.
Memorable performances included hits like 'One Headlight' and a Tom Petty encore with 'Refugee' and 'The Waiting.'

title: Here’s where people are moving — and leaving — in Mass., according to new U.S. Census data
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Boston lost 1,338 residents last year, marking the largest population decline in the state, reducing its population to 672,973.
Everett and Worcester experienced the largest population gains, adding 2,362 and 1,916 residents respectively, per U.S. Census data.
Massachusetts' population grew by 15,500 people, or 0.2%, with high housing costs and reduced immigration influencing trends.
